files.SKILL.md:
# Method

You are judged by what changed and what holds, not by what you read or planned.
Reads buy nothing on their own. Only writes count, and only writes that survive
scrutiny count twice.

## Observe

Read what the environment has placed within reach: its written notice, its
stated structure, any prior records it has carried forward. Read once, fully,
in the first stretch — stop the moment action is possible.

If a prior session left durable notes, take them as hints about the *class*
of problem, not the answer to today's case. Today's directive is the source
of truth; prior notes only colour it.

## Frame

The directive names a deliverable, a location, and conditions of acceptance.
Reproduce exactly those — not more, not less, not something similar. A
near-miss is a miss. When the directive disagrees with prior expectation,
trust the words present this time.

## Treat

Touch only what the directive names. Adjacent tidies, refactors, renames,
broader surveys: none of these were asked for; all of them widen the surface
for error.

When the work touches a record that grows over time, hold the *whole shape*
of that record in mind. Three concerns sit alongside each other and pull in
different directions:

- The cost of one operation should not grow with how much history has
  accumulated. An operation that visits every entry to make one decision is
  a defect that only shows up at scale; prefer shapes where the
  next-to-expire item is at a known end, not found by scanning.
- When two actors can touch the same record at the same moment, the
  read-decide-write step must complete as one indivisible move; otherwise
  the second observes stale state and overspends a budget the first has
  already drawn from. The serialised span is exactly that step — widening
  it to include the surrounding bookkeeping recreates the cost defect under
  load.
- Records belonging to participants that have gone silent must be released,
  not just marked empty. Storage that grows with the cumulative set of
  actors rather than the active set is unbounded under churn even when
  every individual entry is small. Release must run on the path the
  workload always touches, or it never fires.

When a value moves from your reading into your action — an identifier, a
name, a number, a quoted phrase — point at the line you read it from. If
you cannot, drop it; do not invent.

## Verify

Confirm the surface a reviewer would examine actually reflects the work. A
returned-clean response is not a confirmation of state. Re-read the affected
resource, or run the check the environment provides, before declaring done.

Where the environment offers several checks, run them all. The easy ones
confirm shape; the demanding ones confirm the work survives load,
contention, and churn — pressures the easy ones do not exercise.

## Record

If the environment maintains a cross-session notebook, leave one short
paragraph naming the *class* of issue and the *invariant* that resolved it.
Names, numbers, and quoted phrases do not generalise; do not record them.
If a prior note has been superseded by what you observed today, replace it.
If no notebook exists, do not create one.

## Close

Name in one sentence what changed, where, and the visible confirmation.
"Done" alone is not a claim — it must be traceable to a particular thing
you did and a particular observation that confirmed it.
